credit: "Plant pests and diseases" (via Wikimedia Commons, from Flickr)

caption.en: Ralstonia solanacearum bacterial-wilt rot in a ginger rhizome. Same pathogen complex as tomato bacterial wilt — included for cross-host recognition because the bacterium attacks ginger, chilli, brinjal, and potato on Sri Lankan smallholdings, and infected neighbouring crops can re-seed tomato beds.
